fields.description:
## Context
Complete the migration: remove the legacy HMAC operation-cert scheme so both signature types are
asymmetric, handling existing HMAC certificates via an expand-contract migration rather than a
flag-day break.

The migration is captured as a **named observable artifact** (not just "documented"): a file
**`docs/migrations/hmac-opcert-removal.md`** describing the expand-contract cutover window and the
re-issue procedure for existing HMAC certificates, naming the cutover version. Removal deletes
`src/rebar/attest/hmac_legacy.py`'s scheme registration for the op-cert kinds and the HMAC path in
`src/rebar/signing.py` used by plan-review / completion-verifier op-certs, so no code path signs or
accepts an HMAC operation-cert.

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] A test asserts no code path signs or accepts an HMAC operation-cert (the `plan-review` /
      `completion-verifier` kinds no longer resolve to the `HMAC-SHA256` scheme in
      `registry.POLICY`, and the `signing.py` HMAC sign/verify path for op-certs is gone).
- [ ] The migration artifact `docs/migrations/hmac-opcert-removal.md` exists, is non-empty,
      documents the expand-contract cutover window + re-issue procedure, and names the cutover
      version; a test asserts the file exists, is non-empty, and references the cutover — an
      expand-contract migration, not a flag-day break.
- [ ] Rollback: the required-environment policy is opt-in and defaults off (absent
      `.rebar/trusted_environments.yaml` / no `require_environment`); disabling it reverts to local
      behavior. A test asserts the default-off path still produces/reads op-certs locally.
